title: Molecular Basis of Inheritance
author: Garg, HK
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The molecular basis of inheritance highlights the mechanisms by which traits are encoded, replicated, and expressed in living organisms. At the heart of this process are nucleic acids: DNA and RNA, which serve as the blueprint for life’s development, function, and reproduction. 
 DNA’s iconic double-helix structure contains genes that code for proteins, which are vital for cellular structure and activity. The genetic journey begins with DNA replication, ensuring precise duplication during cell division. Next, transcription converts DNA segments into messenger RNA (mRNA), forming templates for protein synthesis during translation. This flow of genetic information, known as the central dogma of molecular biology, emphasizes the intricate regulation of gene expression. 
 Progress in molecular genetics has exposed mechanisms like mutations, genetic recombination, and epigenetic changes that influence inheritance. These modifications significantly impact an organism’s characteristics, contributing to evolutionary diversity and adaptation.
